# Docs & samples sync review

You are reviewing a PR diff for the `microsoft/winappcli` repo and asking:
**do the docs, samples, and shipped Copilot plugin reflect this change?**
Apply the shared output contract in `_shared-contract.md`. Set
`Domain: docs-and-samples` on every finding.

This dimension is mostly read-only research — use `explore` agent type if
available, otherwise standard file reads.

## Docs & sample surfaces

The repo's contributor instructions explicitly call out these places that
must be kept in sync when public-facing features change:

- `docs/usage.md`
- `docs/guides/*.md` (per-framework: dotnet, electron, cpp, rust, flutter,
  tauri, etc.)
- `samples/*` (one directory per framework)
- `docs/fragments/skills/winapp-cli/*` (hand-written; auto-generated into
  `.github/plugin/skills/winapp-cli/*/SKILL.md` by
  `scripts/generate-llm-docs.ps1` via `scripts/build-cli.ps1`)
- `README.md`
- `.github/plugin/agents/winapp.agent.md` (the shipped Copilot agent)
- `docs/cli-schema.json` (autogenerated; flag if commands changed but the
  schema looks stale)

## What to look for

- **New CLI command or new option** added in `src/winapp-CLI/.../Commands/`
  with no corresponding update in:
  - `docs/usage.md`
  - The relevant skill fragment in `docs/fragments/skills/winapp-cli/<area>.md`
  - `.github/plugin/agents/winapp.agent.md` "Complete command reference"
    section (if it's a top-level command worth surfacing to agents)
  - Any framework guide that previously documented an adjacent command
- **Changed command behavior** (option semantics, defaults, error messages,
  output format) without updating docs that describe the prior behavior.
- **New sample directory** (`samples/<new-framework>/`) without:
  - A README explaining the sample
  - A corresponding guide in `docs/guides/`
  - An entry in framework lists in top-level `README.md`
  - A `test.Tests.ps1` (this is also a test-coverage finding)
- **Broken cross-links.** New docs that link to renamed or deleted files;
  removed docs still referenced from `README.md` or other guides.
- **Skill description drift.** If a new top-level command warrants a new
  shipped skill, check whether `$SkillDescriptions` in
  `scripts/generate-llm-docs.ps1` needs an entry.
- **Stale autogenerated artifacts.** If `Commands/` changed but the diff
  doesn't include a regenerated `docs/cli-schema.json`, flag it (don't run
  the script — just note the mismatch). Same for
  `src/winapp-npm/src/winapp-commands.ts` — auto-generated, regenerate via
  `npm run generate-commands` (see repo memory).
- **Agent file currency.** `.github/plugin/agents/winapp.agent.md` has a
  command-selection decision tree and a complete command reference — a new
  command must appear in both.
- **Critical-rules updates.** When a behavior changes that contradicts a rule
  listed in `winapp.agent.md` "Critical rules", that rule needs updating.
- **VSC extension labels.** New VS Code commands should follow the
  `"category": "WinApp"` convention and avoid `"WinApp:"` prefixes in titles
  (see repo memory).

## What to drop

- Asking for grammar tweaks unrelated to the change.
- Asking to update docs for behavior that didn't change.
- Flagging the autogenerated `cli-schema.json` / `winapp-commands.ts`
  themselves as needing edits — they regenerate; only flag if the diff
  forgot to regenerate.

## Severity guide for this dimension

- New user-visible command/option missing from `docs/usage.md` and the
  relevant skill fragment → high.
- Behavior change that contradicts a rule in `winapp.agent.md` → high.
- New sample without guide / README → medium.
- Stale `cli-schema.json` / `winapp-commands.ts` → medium (will be caught by
  build, but better to fix before push).
- Polish (typo, link target moved) → low.
